---
title: "02_summary_table"
author: "Hugo Maruyama"
date: "2026-03-10"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Introduction

This section summarizes the sequencing throughput achieved by the Illumina MiSeq and Oxford Nanopore Technologies (ONT) platforms. Comparing the average number of high-quality reads per sample is essential for assessing the depth of coverage and the overall sensitivity of the comparative analysis.

## Sequencing Summary (Table 1)

In this analysis, non-chimeric read counts from the Illumina pipeline and filtered read counts from the ONT EPI2ME `wf-16s` workflow were utilized. These counts reflect the data quality after rigorous quality control and filtering processes.

**Note:** The following table corresponds to **Table 1** in the main manuscript (Exported as `Table1_read_summary.png`).

```{r}
#| label: sequencing-summary

library(tidyverse)
library(gt)

# Define the read counts for each sample after quality control and filtering.
# Illumina: counts of non-chimeric reads.
# ONT: read counts extracted from the EPI2ME wf-16S report.
illumina_reads <- c(79689, 95146, 88794, 69149)
ont_reads <- c(102299, 107381, 121916, 87402)

# Create a summary data frame calculating mean and standard deviation.
read_summary_data <- tibble(
  Platform = c("Illumina MiSeq", "Oxford Nanopore"),
  Mean_Reads = c(mean(illumina_reads), mean(ont_reads)),
  SD_Reads = c(sd(illumina_reads), sd(ont_reads))
) %>%
  # Format the values for academic presentation, including comma separators for readability.
  dplyr::mutate(
    `Mean Reads per Sample (± SD)` = paste0(
      format(round(Mean_Reads, 0), big.mark = ","),
      " ± ",
      format(round(SD_Reads, 0), big.mark = ",")
    )
  ) %>%
  # Select the columns required for the final summary table.
  dplyr::select(Platform, `Mean Reads per Sample (± SD)`)

# Generate a professionally styled table using the 'gt' package.
read_summary_table_gt <- read_summary_data %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Table 1: Summary of Sequencing Reads per Sample"
  ) %>%
  # Center-align all columns for a balanced layout.
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = everything()
  ) %>%
  # Apply formatting options consistent with academic publication standards.
  tab_options(
    column_labels.font.weight = "bold",
    table_body.hlines.style = "none",
    column_labels.border.bottom.style = "solid",
    column_labels.border.bottom.width = px(2),
    table.border.top.style = "none",
    table.border.bottom.style = "none"
  )

# Export the table for use in external documents.
if (!dir.exists("tables")) {
  dir.create("tables")
}

gtsave(read_summary_table_gt, "tables/Table1_read_summary.png")
gtsave(read_summary_table_gt, "tables/Table1_read_summary.rtf")

# Render the table within the document.
read_summary_table_gt
```

## Observations

The summary statistics demonstrate the comparative sequencing depth available for each method. Ensuring sufficient read depth is critical for the reliable detection of low-abundance taxa and the accurate characterization of the microbial community structure.
